title: Song - Love While You May.
content:
Day by day, with startling fleetness,
        Life speeds away;
    Love, alone, can glean its sweetness,
        Love while you may.
    While the soul is strong and fearless,
    While the eye is bright and tearless,
    Ere the heart is chilled and cheerless - 
        Love while you may.

    Life may pass, but love, undying,
        Dreads no decay;
    Even from the grave replying,
        "Love while you may."
    Love's the fruit, as life's the flower;
    Love is heaven's rarest dower;
    Love gives love its quick'ning power - 
        Love while you may.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles Sangster, titled "Song - Love While You May.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
